{{Quote|The Siege of Trasemene! The Assault on Vardoss IV! The Battle of Galboron! Names that will forever live in the annals of Imperial military history… and now you can see if you have the brilliance to match those who fought there!|Advertisement for the [[CS-Mark 10]] in the [[Galladinium's 50th anniversary edition|fiftieth-anniversary edition]] of ''[[Galladinium's Galactic Datalog of Fantastic Technology]]''|<ref name="Galladinium">''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]''</ref>}}
'''Galboron''' was a celestial body<ref name="EGTW" /> situated in the [[Galboron system/Legends|Galboron system]], a part of the [[Hertae sector/Legends|Hertae sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It and the [[Nexus Ortai system/Legends|Nexus Ortai system]] were both located near the intersection of the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] known as<ref name="Routes" /> the [[Askar Trace]]<ref name="SoT">''[[Stay on Target]]''</ref>—which linked them to the [[Leritor system/Legends|Leritor]], [[Lanos system/Legends|Lanos]], and [[Bresnia system/Legends|Bresnia system]]—and the [[Reena Trade Route]], which connected them to the [[Masterra system/Legends|Masterra]] and [[Spirador system/Legends|Spirador systems]].<ref name="Routes" />

In [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]], the [[Noolian Crisis]] began when [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] members of the [[Separatist holdouts/Legends|holdouts]] of the recently defeated [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] seized Galboron and other nearby [[Planet/Legends|worlds]]. The conflict was resolved when the Aqualish were [[Reconquest of the Rim|defeated]] by a [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Task force/Legends|task force]] at<ref name="EGTW" /> the famous<ref name="Galladinium" /> [[Battle of Galboron]].<ref name="EGTW" />

==Behind the scenes==
Galboron was first introduced as part of the name of the Battle of Galboron in ''[[Galladinium's Fantastic Technology]]'', a [[1995]] sourcebook authored by [[Rick D. Stuart]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Galladinium" /> In [[2012]], the reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', authored by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Paul R. Urquhart]], established Galboron as a distinct astronomical object.<ref name="EGTW" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Galboron [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body itself, in grid square Q-14.<ref name="TEA" />
